brief:
GAP/WHY: miR1 (microRNA-1), encoded by miR1-1 and miR1-2 genes, is the most abundant cardiac microRNA, critical for heart development/physiology; dysregulation linked to heart disease. Patient hearts with atrial fibrillation or acute MI show >75% reduction in miR1 expression. Unknown whether miR1 deficiency is a PRIMARY causal (etiological) factor of cardiac remodeling vs a downstream consequence.

DESIGN/SETTING: Combined in vivo (mouse) + cellular/molecular mechanistic study. Engineered genetic mouse model plus rescue experiment.

MODEL/SAMPLE: miR1-1 or miR1-2 knockout mice crossbred to generate 75%-miR1-knockdown (75%KD) mice with genotypes miR1-1+/-:miR1-2-/- OR miR1-1-/-:miR1-2+/-. Wild-type (WT) as comparator. Cardiomyocytes and whole hearts assessed.

METHODS/ASSAYS: ECG, patch clamping, optical mapping, transcriptomics (RNA sequencing), proteomics.

INTERVENTION/RESCUE: 6 weeks of bi-weekly intravenous tail-vein injection of miR1 mimics in 75%KD mice.

KEY RESULTS (exact numbers):
- In adult 75%KD hearts, total miR1 expression reduced to approx 25% of normal WT level.
- Patient context cited: >75% reduction in miR1 in AF or acute MI hearts.
- Cardiac function: 75%KD hearts showed decreased ejection fraction and decreased fractional shortening.
- ECG: prolonged QRS and prolonged QT intervals; high susceptibility to arrhythmias.
- Cellular electrophysiology: 75%KD cardiomyocytes had prolonged action potentials with impaired repolarization and impaired excitation-contraction coupling.
- Ion currents vs WT: REDUCED Na+ current and REDUCED transient outward potassium current; ELEVATED L-type Ca2+ current.
- Omics: RNA-seq + proteomics showed negative regulation of cardiac muscle contraction and ion channel activities, plus positive enrichment of smooth muscle contraction genes in 75%KD.
- miR1 deficiency dysregulated a wide gene network; miR1 RNAi-direct targets influenced many indirectly regulated genes.
- Rescue: after 6 weeks bi-weekly IV miR1 mimic injection, ejection fraction and fractional shortening of 75%KD hearts significantly improved BUT hearts remained susceptible to arrhythmias.

TAKEAWAY: miR1 deficiency acts as a PRIMARY etiological factor inducing cardiac remodeling by disrupting heart regulatory homeostasis. Achieving stable and appropriate microRNA expression levels is critical for effective miRNA-based therapy in cardiovascular disease (partial rescue underscores narrow therapeutic window).
